narrative: As an Asian male applicant with an upper-middle-class family background and plans to attend law school full-time, your profile stood out in several ways, including your undergraduate GPA of 2.60—which is below the typical 3.21 average—and your LSAT score of 34, compared to the usual 36.70. The admissions model evaluated your chances of passing the bar exam on the first try, predicting a 58% probability that you would fail, which crossed the threshold for rejection to protect the school's bar passage rates. This decision hinged most heavily on your Year 3 school decile ranking of 2, well below the average of 5.53, as it was the strongest signal pointing toward potential bar exam struggles, reflecting weaker performance relative to peers at that stage. Your LSAT score played a notable counterbalancing role, pushing the prediction toward success because it was reasonably competitive, even if not at the top tier. Similarly, your undergraduate GPA offered some positive influence by suggesting a baseline academic capability, despite being lower than average. Overall, your Year 3 decile outweighed these strengths, making the model view your bar exam risk as too high compared to typical admitted students, who often rank in the middle or upper deciles by that point. While your full-time status aligns with the vast majority of students (over 92%), it didn't sway the outcome much here. This isn't a judgment on your potential as an Asian male from a solid background—many in similar circumstances succeed with targeted improvements. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost your score or strengthening your later academic record through postgraduate courses or a master's program to improve your decile standing. Building a narrative around any upward trends in your grades could also help in reapplying. Reach out to admissions advisors for feedback, and explore bridge programs designed for students with non-traditional paths. With focused effort, you can reshape how models like this see your future bar exam odds.
